event_description: An airborne camera tracks a white pickup truck (marked 'TERRASENSE') performing aggressive, high-friction circular drifting maneuvers ('donuts') in an empty parking lot. The EO view shows the truck's physical movement and faint, dark tire skid marks on the pavement. The IR view reveals highly distinct, bright (hot) transient thermal tracks left behind by the tires, representing the heat generated by the intense friction against the asphalt, alongside the expected bright engine hotspot.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with truck path and friction-heated tire tracks; the hot tire traces left behind nearby
options.B: the object with the hot tire traces left behind; truck path and friction-heated tire tracks nearby
options.C: the object with lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; roadside shadows or field texture nearby
options.D: the object with roadside shadows or field texture; road-following motion through scale changes nearby

qa.evidence_note: the truck performs donuts and leaves faint dark tire skid marks

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which motion cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: the hot tire traces left behind; view scale or angle changes
options.B: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; view scale or angle changes
options.C: roadside shadows or field texture; view scale or angle changes
options.D: truck path and friction-heated tire tracks; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the truck performs donuts and leaves faint dark tire skid marks

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings
options.B: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; the hot tire traces left behind
options.C: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; truck path and friction-heated tire tracks
options.D: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; roadside shadows or field texture

qa.evidence_note: bright transient tire-heat tracks appear along with the engine hotspot

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: roadside shadows or field texture; near the object
options.B: truck path and friction-heated tire tracks; near the object
options.C: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; near the object
options.D: the hot tire traces left behind; near the object

qa.evidence_note: treating the hot tire traces as separate moving objects

options.C: EO: the hot tire traces left behind; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.D: EO: truck path and friction-heated tire tracks; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background

qa.evidence_note: EO: the truck performs donuts and leaves faint dark tire skid marks; IR: bright transient tire-heat tracks appear along with the engine hotspot

options.C: the object: truck path and friction-heated tire tracks; screen scale or angle changes

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
